faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions about criminal defense

items.q: What should I do if I was detained by police?
items.a: Demand a lawyer immediately and do not give any statements without one. Call me right away — I will come and provide your defense from the very first minute of detention.

items.q: Is it possible to be released from custody before trial?
items.a: Yes. I challenge the legality of detention and prepare motions to change the preventive measure to bail or house arrest. In most of my cases, clients are released.

items.q: How much does criminal defense cost?
items.a: The cost depends on the complexity of the case and the scope of work. The first consultation is free. Afterward I will give you an accurate estimate and we will find the best option together.

items.q: Do you take cases where the prosecution has a strong case?
items.a: Those are exactly the cases I specialize in. Even with a large evidence base, there are always procedural violations or mitigating circumstances that can and must be used.

items.q: How quickly can you take on my case?
items.a: Urgently. I accept cases without delay. If my presence is needed today — I will be there today.

cta.title: Does Your Situation Require Immediate Defense?
cta.subtitle: Every hour at the start of a case can change its outcome. Don't wait.

items.title: Defense in Murder & Serious Crime Cases
items.desc: Defense in cases involving murder, bodily harm, robbery, armed robbery. Full support from detention to verdict.

items.title: Drug-Related Offenses
items.desc: Defense in cases involving possession, distribution, and production of narcotics. Verification of operative and investigative measures.

items.title: Economic Crimes
items.desc: Fraud, embezzlement, tax evasion, money laundering. Defense for entrepreneurs and officials.

items.title: Corruption Cases
items.desc: Defense in bribery, abuse of office, and corruption cases. Specialization in NABU and SAP cases.

items.title: Appeals & Sentence Challenges
items.desc: Appealing verdicts in appellate and Supreme courts. Reopening cases based on newly discovered circumstances.

items.title: Pre-trial Detention & Bail
items.desc: Challenging the legality of arrest, motions for bail, house arrest, and change of preventive measure.

items.title: Legal Consultations & Analysis
items.desc: Legal consultation on criminal matters, case material analysis, risk assessment, and defense strategy.

about.title: About the Attorney
about.name: Yulia Moskalenko
about.bio: For over 30 years I have devoted myself to defending the rights of people in criminal cases of any complexity. From the early steps in my career to high-profile resonant cases — I always put the client's interests first.
about.bio2: I believe every person deserves a fair defense, regardless of the nature of the charge. My duty is to ensure that right with maximum dedication and professionalism.

items.text: Graduated with honors from Taras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv, Faculty of Law
Obtained attorney's certificate, admitted to the Ukrainian Bar
Practice at leading legal firms, developing specialization in criminal law
Opened own practice, first landmark acquittal verdict
Successful application to the European Court of Human Rights — a precedent in defendant rights protection
Over 500 successfully closed cases, 97% positive outcomes, recognized among Ukraine's top criminal defense attorneys

achievements.title: Achievements
items: Acquittals in cases with maximum sentencing
Successful appeals in the Supreme Court of Ukraine
Cases before the European Court of Human Rights (ECHR)
Defense in NABU and Special Anti-Corruption Prosecutor cases
Member of the Kyiv Regional Bar Council
Speaker at international criminal law conferences
